modules.name: project-dir
modules.responsibility: PROJECT_DIR and SCHEMAS_DIR constants — single source for .project/ path (renamed from .workflow/ during monorepo restructure)

modules.name: schema-validator
modules.responsibility: AJV wrapper: validate(), validateFromFile(), ValidationError class with formatted messages

modules.name: block-api
modules.responsibility: Centralized block I/O: readBlock(), writeBlock(), appendToBlock(), updateItemInBlock() with write-time schema validation and atomic writes against .project/*.json files

modules.name: block-validation
modules.responsibility: Post-step block validation: snapshotBlockFiles(), validateChangedBlocks() with rollback support for .project/*.json files

modules.name: project-sdk
modules.responsibility: Project state SDK: availableBlocks(), availableSchemas(), findAppendableBlocks(), projectState() — all derived dynamically from filesystem and git. Moved from workflow-sdk during monorepo split.

modules.name: pi-project-index
modules.responsibility: Extension entry point for pi-project: registers append-block-item tool, update-block-item tool, and /project command (status, add-work). Extracted from pi-workflows index during monorepo split.

modules.name: types
modules.responsibility: All shared interfaces: WorkflowSpec, StepSpec, AgentSpec, ExecutionState, StepResult, WorkflowResult, CompletionSpec, ExpressionScope, LoopSpec, GateSpec, TransformSpec, RetryConfig, ArtifactSpec

modules.name: format
modules.responsibility: Shared formatDuration/formatCost/formatTokens utilities

modules.name: template
modules.responsibility: Nunjucks three-tier rendering (project > user > builtin), ${{ }} protection from Nunjucks interpretation

modules.name: expression
modules.responsibility: ${{ }} expression evaluator: property access, pipe filters (duration, currency, json, length, keys, filter), resolveExpressions(), evaluateCondition() for when: clauses

modules.name: state
modules.responsibility: Run directory initialization, state persistence via atomic write (tmp + rename), metrics tracking, generateRunId(), buildResult(), formatResult()

modules.name: tui
modules.responsibility: Progress widget with token display, step status, timing, cost, StepActivity and StepOutputSummary tracking

modules.name: output
modules.responsibility: persistStepOutput(): writes all outputs as JSON, supports author-declared output.path

modules.name: step-shared
modules.responsibility: Shared utilities: zeroUsage(), addUsage(), buildPrompt(), persistStep(), compileAgentSpec(), resolveSchemaPath(), constants (SIGKILL_GRACE_MS, WIDGET_ID, DEFAULT_MAX_ATTEMPTS)

modules.name: agent-spec
modules.responsibility: YAML agent spec loader: parseAgentYaml, createAgentLoader, three-tier search (.pi/agents/ > ~/.pi/agent/agents/ > packages/pi-workflows/agents/), AgentNotFoundError, AgentParseError

modules.name: dispatch
modules.responsibility: Subprocess spawn: pi --mode json, model override, tool filtering, extension/skill scoping, session log capture, usage accumulation, ModelConfig resolution

modules.name: completion
modules.responsibility: Completion field resolution: template and message+include forms, resolves against CompletionScope

modules.name: dag
modules.responsibility: Dependency graph: buildPlanFromDeps(), conservative plan adds implicit sequential deps for steps without explicit refs, extractDependencies(), isSequential()

modules.name: workflow-sdk
modules.responsibility: Workflow SDK facade: vocabulary (filterNames, stepTypes, expressionRoots), discovery (agents, templates, schemas, workflows), introspection (extractExpressions, declaredSteps, declaredAgentRefs, declaredSchemaRefs). Project state functions (projectState, availableBlocks, findAppendableBlocks) moved to pi-project/project-sdk.

modules.name: checkpoint
modules.responsibility: findIncompleteRun(), validateResumeCompatibility(), computeResumePoint(), formatIncompleteRun() for crash recovery and resume

modules.name: step-pause
modules.responsibility: Pause step: returns completed immediately, executor sets status=paused

modules.name: step-transform
modules.responsibility: Pure expression mapping: no LLM invocation, resolves mapping fields via ${{ }}

modules.name: step-gate
modules.responsibility: Async gate execution with process groups, onPass/onFail handling, shell command pass/fail based on exit code

modules.name: step-command
modules.responsibility: Shell command execution: sh -c, captures stdout as JSON or text, fails on non-zero exit

modules.name: step-agent
modules.responsibility: Agent dispatch with injectable dispatchFn, template compilation, output path resolution, output schema validation, RetryContext support

modules.name: step-foreach
modules.responsibility: forEach iteration: resolves array, binds element via as, adds forEach.index/length to scope, collects outputs

modules.name: step-loop
modules.responsibility: Loop iteration with accumulated evidence, receives dispatchAgent callback, onExhausted handling, gate and transform execution

modules.name: step-parallel
modules.responsibility: executeParallelLayer() + executeParallelStep(), receives executeSingleStep callback, concurrent execution with Promise.allSettled

modules.name: workflow-spec
modules.responsibility: YAML parsing: WorkflowSpec from .workflow.yaml files, including completion field and artifacts, WorkflowSpecError class

modules.name: workflow-discovery
modules.responsibility: Directory scanning for .workflow.yaml: project (.pi/workflows/) and user (~/.pi/agent/workflows/), discoverWorkflows(), findWorkflow()

modules.name: workflow-executor
modules.responsibility: Main orchestration: buildConservativePlan(), executeSingleStep(), executeWorkflow(), pause flag, DAG layering, block validation integration. Cross-package dependency on pi-project for schema validation and block I/O.

modules.name: pi-workflows-index
modules.responsibility: Extension entry point for pi-workflows: registers workflow tool, /workflow command (run, list, resume), keybindings (Ctrl+H pause, Ctrl+J resume). Block tools and /project command moved to pi-project/index.

patterns.name: subprocess-dispatch
patterns.description: Each workflow step spawns pi --mode json as a child process. The main pi instance orchestrates, never yields its own session.

patterns.name: expression-resolution
patterns.description: ${{ }} expressions resolve via property access only — no eval(), no arithmetic. Pipe filters for formatting.

patterns.name: three-tier-search
patterns.description: Resources resolved: .pi/<dir>/ > ~/.pi/agent/<dir>/ > packages/pi-workflows/<dir>/. Used for agents, templates, and workflows.

patterns.name: atomic-state-write
patterns.description: State persisted after each step via tmp file + rename for crash safety.

patterns.name: injectable-dispatch
patterns.description: Step executors receive dispatch functions as callbacks for testability without subprocesses.

patterns.name: conservative-plan
patterns.description: buildConservativePlan() adds implicit sequential deps for steps without explicit ${{ steps.X }} refs. Parallelism is opt-in.

patterns.name: nunjucks-template-composition
patterns.description: Agent prompts compiled through Nunjucks unconditionally. {% extends %}/{% block %} for template inheritance. ${{ }} protected from Nunjucks.

patterns.name: block-validation-rollback
patterns.description: Post-step validation of .project/*.json files with snapshot-based rollback on schema violation.

boundaries: Two-package monorepo: pi-project owns block I/O, schema validation, and project state; pi-workflows owns workflow orchestration, step execution, and agent dispatch
pi-workflows depends on pi-project (cross-package imports for schema-validator, block-validation, block-api, project-dir) — pi-project has no dependency on pi-workflows
Extension owns subprocess dispatch — no dependency on pi-subagents
types.ts is the single source for all shared workflow interfaces — no type definitions elsewhere
Step executors (step-*.ts) receive callbacks, never import workflow-executor directly
Template engine (template.ts) has no dependency on workflow types — pure Nunjucks wrapper
schema-validator.ts is a standalone AJV wrapper — no workflow-specific logic
block-validation.ts operates independently — snapshot/validate/rollback with no workflow state coupling
pi-project index.ts registers block tools and /project command; pi-workflows index.ts registers workflow tool and /workflow command — separate extension entry points

compilation_pipeline.description: YAML spec + Nunjucks templates + resolved input → rendered .md → pi subprocess. Agent specs are YAML (.agent.yaml). They declare input schema, output schema, model, tools, role, and template references (prompt.system, prompt.task). The .md that pi consumes is compiled at dispatch time from spec + templates + typed input data. It exists in memory for milliseconds — written to a temp file, passed to pi --append-system-prompt, deleted. It is never a hand-authored source file.

stages.name: YAML spec parsing
stages.description: parseAgentYaml loads and validates the agent spec declaring input schema, output schema, model, tools, role, and template references

stages.name: Template resolution
stages.description: Three-tier search resolves template files: .pi/templates/ > ~/.pi/agent/templates/ > packages/pi-workflows/templates/

stages.name: Template rendering
stages.input: Nunjucks template + resolved typed input data
stages.description: compileAgentSpec() renders both system and task prompts through Nunjucks unconditionally. No hasTemplateSyntax gate — every prompt goes through the template engine. Plain text renders to itself. ${{ }} workflow expressions are protected from Nunjucks interpretation.

stages.name: Subprocess dispatch
stages.input: Rendered .md prompt + agent config (model, tools, extensions, skills)
stages.output: StepResult with output, usage, session log
stages.description: Prompt written to temp file, passed to pi --mode json as a child process. Process exit is the definitive completion signal.

compilation_pipeline.intermediate_representation: JSON is the intermediate representation between steps. Every step produces outputs/<stepName>.json (or an author-declared path via output.path). Structured data written directly; string output wrapped as { "text": "..." }. Downstream agents consume typed JSON fields via templates — not string pastes.

execution_model.description: Conservative plan, layer-based execution with resume support, nine step types, three-tier resolution, pause/resume mechanisms.
execution_model.conservative_plan: buildConservativePlan() adds implicit sequential deps for steps without explicit ${{ steps.X }} refs. Parallelism is opt-in.
execution_model.layer_execution: Processes layers of the dependency graph. Filters to pending steps within each layer. Partial parallel layers resume correctly — only pending steps in each layer execute.

three_tier_resolution.pattern: .pi/<dir>/ > ~/.pi/agent/<dir>/ > packages/pi-workflows/<dir>/ — project-level overrides user-level overrides package defaults

mechanisms: Workflow-authored: pause: step type returns completed immediately, executor sets status=paused
User-initiated: Ctrl+H pauses after current step completes
Crash recovery: auto-detect incomplete runs on /workflow run and workflow tool

dispatch_design.method: Subprocess spawn via pi --mode json. Each step spawns a separate pi process. The main pi instance orchestrates — parsing specs, validating schemas, resolving expressions, dispatching subprocesses, collecting results — but never yields its own session or agent loop.
dispatch_design.rationale: Independence from pi-subagents — two tools can coexist without shared state or import relationship. The user never loses their interactive session. When a workflow completes, the result surfaces in the main conversation.
dispatch_design.alternative_considered: In-process dispatch via newSession() + sendMessage() within the same pi instance, with the workflow owning the agent loop (GSD-2 pattern).
dispatch_design.alternative_rationale: Sacrifices the user interactive session — each unit wipes the context, and the user becomes an observer. Exists as an optimization for long-running autonomous execution where subprocess overhead per step is wasteful and mid-turn supervision (steering messages) is valuable. Could be added later as explicit opt-in escalation.
dispatch_design.parallelism_model: Sequential steps run one subprocess at a time. Parallel steps (independent steps with no data dependencies) spawn multiple subprocesses concurrently. Falls out naturally from the subprocess model — parallelism is just multiple processes, no special execution mode needed.
